title: exocarpos syrticola
intro: Exocarpos syrticola, also known as the Western Australian quandong or quandong, is a small shrub native to Western Australia. This plant has been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Exocarpos syrticola include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support wound healing. Exocarpos syrticola is typically harvested for its fruit, which is then used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and jams.
